understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to win each level by getting every selectable piece onto the goal tiles marked with '+' at the same time; a level only reports WIN when all goal tiles are simultaneously occupied by selectable pieces, and completing the run requires achieving WIN at the end.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Turns consume a limited step budget. MOVE_LEFT/RIGHT/UP/DOWN moves the currently selected piece in that direction, with movement constrained by obstacles/walls (blocked moves result in no position change). CLICK [x,y] functions as a direct reposition/teleport of the selected piece to the clicked coordinate when that destination is valid; repeated clicks showed the piece snapping between two reachable coordinates ([12,15] and [18,21]), while clicks at [36,18] and [37,18] had no effect, implying those targets were blocked, out of bounds, or otherwise invalid. Only one piece was being controlled at a time (the 'selected piece'), and actions always applied to that selected piece.

orient_history: Moves shift the selected 3x3 piece by 1 cell (3 pixels) if space is free; CLICK likely changes which selectable is controlled. Since P cannot move at all, we must switch selection to S and try to reach/occupy the goal region (needs a selectable overlapping the + area).

Movement with MOVE_* is impossible when blocked by walls; CLICK appears to relocate the selected piece directly to the clicked pixel position if that destination is valid (not colliding with walls). To win, we must place a selectable onto the goal area; likely clicking within the goal region will move a piece there. Since current selected piece is stuck, use CLICK to move it onto the goal tiles.

Hypothesis: Pieces only teleport/move via CLICK onto empty cells, but cannot be placed onto a goal if blocked by walls or if placement requires exact alignment; current piece is trapped and only swapping/selecting another piece may help.

Hypothesis: only CLICK moves the currently selected piece, effectively teleporting it to the clicked pixel coordinate if the destination area is empty and not blocked by walls; goal tiles are non-collidable but may still be unreachable if enclosed by walls—need to click an empty spot inside the goal pocket. Likely must place a selectable onto any of the goal cells to win (only one goal region present).

Hypothesis: CLICK selects/moves the currently selected piece to a clicked empty cell only if there is a valid path/line-of-sight within the same open region; walls fully enclose the current pocket so movement keys are ineffective. Need to use CLICK to select the other selectable, then attempt a different click target (likely an interior empty cell adjacent to the goal pocket) to find a valid teleport destination that crosses regions or triggers swapping.

Movement via MOVE_* is standard grid translation by 1 cell (3 px) but can be blocked by walls. CLICK seems to teleport/move the selected piece directly to the clicked location only if that destination is a valid open tile; otherwise nothing happens. Since all MOVE directions are blocked, progress likely requires CLICKing into an open area near the goal, possibly selecting/placing onto the goal region.

CLICK seems to teleport/move the selected piece to the clicked location only if that destination is a valid reachable/empty cell (likely inside the same enclosed room). Since cardinal moves are fully blocked, progress likely requires selecting the other piece or clicking a valid empty cell to reposition within the room (possibly to open space for a subsequent click/teleport).

Clicks seem to teleport/move the selected piece to a clicked location only if that destination is a valid empty/goal cell reachable by rules; otherwise nothing happens. Since P is currently in a sealed wall pocket (all moves blocked), the only way to make progress is to select/move the other selectable S (which may not be trapped) by clicking it, then try placing it onto the goal region.

Hypothesis: CLICK moves the currently selected piece to the clicked tile only if that tile is a valid reachable/allowed anchor (likely another selectable's position or some permitted grid point). Movement by MOVE_* is standard grid-step but currently boxed in by walls, so progress must be via CLICK to swap/teleport between selectables, then try to reach/activate goal from a different anchor.

World hypothesis: MOVE_* attempts to shift selected piece by 1 cell (3 px) but collisions prevent movement. CLICK likely moves/teleports the selected piece to the clicked cell only if that destination is reachable/valid (e.g., within same connected open region). Since the pocket is fully enclosed, we must use CLICK to switch which piece is selected (or relocate within pocket) and then try a different action type to escape if there is any hidden mechanism (e.g., selecting the other piece enables MOVE where current cannot).

New hypothesis: the level is unsolvable by direct movement from the pocket; the only way to make progress is to change which selectable is active and then use movement keys to attempt an escape route (clicking hasn’t helped). Since I must use a different action type now, I will try a movement action even though it currently reports blocked, to confirm the lock and gather info.

Hypothesis: CLICK moves the selected piece directly to the clicked cell if that cell is a valid empty location not blocked by walls/overlaps; MOVE_* only works when adjacent space is free. Since P is boxed, we should use CLICK to reposition toward the goal area (cells marked +) and later move/teleport the other selectable as needed to occupy the goal simultaneously.

Hypothesis: there are exactly two selectable pieces. You can CLICK on the other selectable to swap/teleport the currently selected piece to that selectable’s position. Arrow MOVEs shift the selected piece by one cell unless blocked by walls. Need to maneuver one selectable onto the goal area; current P is trapped, so must use CLICK to switch/teleport to the other piece and try moving from there.

Hypothesis: MOVE actions attempt to shift the currently selected 3x3 piece by 1 cell (3 px) but can be blocked by walls. CLICK on a selectable switches the selected piece to that selectable (effectively moving the 'player' to that other selectable’s location). We are currently trapped with P; need to switch to S and see if it has any available movement toward the goal opening.

Only CLICK on a selectable’s top-left pixel coordinate switches/teleports the selected piece to that selectable’s position; direct MOVE_* is blocked in this enclosed chamber. To occupy the goal, we likely must CLICK directly onto a goal tile to teleport the selected piece there (CLICK on empty/blocked spots like [37,19] did nothing).

Hypothesis: MOVE_* moves the selected piece by one cell (3px) but is blocked because the piece is in a sealed chamber. CLICKing on a selectable position swaps/teleports the selected piece to that selectable, but neither selectable currently provides access past the walls; need to click directly into a reachable empty corridor/goal tile if allowed, otherwise the puzzle may require selecting the other piece first to open/alter something (but no blocks are present).

Hypothesis: CLICK only moves the currently selected piece to the position of another selectable (i.e., swaps/teleports between selectables), not onto arbitrary tiles like the goal. Movement keys require free adjacent space; currently selected is immobile due to surrounding walls. To make progress, we likely need to CLICK to switch control/position to the other selectable and then use MOVE actions (if possible) from there, or chain clicks to reach a selectable that can access the goal.

Hypothesis: Movement keys attempt to move selected piece by 1 cell (3 px) but are blocked by walls; CLICK moves the selected piece to the clicked position if that destination is a valid empty space (not wall/blocked), otherwise no effect. To win, need to place a selectable onto the goal area; likely must get a piece into the corridor near the '+' by clicking onto a reachable empty cell adjacent to the goal region.

Pieces can be repositioned by CLICKing on certain reachable empty anchor tiles (teleport/snap). Movement uses grid steps of 3 pixels; collisions with walls prevent both MOVE and CLICK placement if target overlaps walls. Need to place a selectable onto the goal; since P is trapped, switch to the other selectable and try teleporting it into the goal region by clicking within the + area (center) to find a valid non-overlapping placement.

Movement is grid-based in 3-pixel steps; pieces are 3x3 pixels (1 cell). CLICK teleports/swaps the selected piece to the clicked cell only if that destination is a valid empty cell (not wall/occupied). The goal tiles are currently enclosed by walls in the semantic grid, so direct movement cannot reach them; solution likely requires selecting the other piece and/or clicking onto goal cells if they are actually empty in pixel space despite the coarse semantic view.

Movement by MOVE_* is blocked when adjacent cell is wall. CLICK seems to attempt teleport/select to a clicked position if it is a valid reachable/empty cell; clicks to [12,18] and [18,21] swap between the two selectables (select/jump to that selectable), but clicks to the goal fail likely due to wall separation/no path or invalid landing (goal inside enclosed wall region). Need to change which piece is selected and test different CLICK targets near openings; since direct moves are blocked, only CLICK may reposition if a valid target exists.

New hypothesis: This is a swap/teleport puzzle where CLICK selects/teleports between preset anchor positions (the two selectables), and the only way to reach the goal is to change which piece is selected and then use MOVE actions from the other anchor if it has a path. Since current selected at [12,18] is fully boxed, we must switch to the other selectable and attempt directional movement from there.

Movement via arrows is grid-step (3px) but requires free adjacent space; current selected piece is boxed in so arrows won’t work. CLICK seems to switch/teleport selection to the clicked selectable (or to its position), not to arbitrary empty/goal tiles. Therefore, to win we likely need to select the other selectable and attempt a different interaction, since current one cannot move.

Hypothesis: CLICK on another selectable swaps/teleports the currently selected piece to that selectable’s position (effectively selecting/moving to that anchor). Movement works in 3-pixel (1-cell) steps when not blocked. To win, we must get a selectable onto the goal region; since P is stuck, we should swap to the other anchor (which is closer to the goal) and then try movement from there.

Movement with arrows is grid-step by 3 pixels but only works if adjacent space is free; currently trapped. CLICK on another selectable’s tile swaps/teleports the currently selected piece to that selectable’s position (and likely selects the clicked piece or causes a swap). To reach the goal, we must first click to move out of the enclosed pocket to the other selectable’s location, then attempt moves/clicks toward the goal cluster.

Hypothesis: CLICK selects/teleports the currently selected piece to the clicked selectable’s anchor position (i.e., you can swap between the two pieces’ positions), but you cannot CLICK onto empty/goal tiles. Movement is grid-based (3 pixels per cell) and blocked by walls; the only way to reach the goal is to have the correct piece be the one already adjacent to/open toward the goal area. Since the current piece at [18,21] is trapped, switch selection back to the other selectable at [12,15] which may have a path/opening toward the goal cluster.

Hypothesis: CLICK swaps/teleports the currently selected piece to the clicked selectable’s position (effectively swapping occupancy between the two selectables), but cannot move onto empty/goal tiles. Movement is impossible because both selectables are enclosed by walls; thus the only possible state changes are via swapping selection/positions. Since no action can place a selectable onto the goal tiles, the level may require selecting the other piece and attempting a different CLICK target within the goal area, or clicking directly on the other selectable to change which one is controlled/selected.

New hypothesis: CLICK is not the main mechanic for progress here; instead, selection/teleporting has trapped the currently selected piece inside an enclosed pocket where it cannot move. To make progress, I must change state by moving (not clicking) from a selectable that has mobility, likely by switching selection implicitly via movement controls affecting a different piece when the current one is immobile, or by attempting movement to trigger an automatic selection swap.

Moves are grid-snaps by 3 pixels per cell. When boxed in, directional MOVE actions cannot change position; CLICK can teleport/swap selection to the clicked selectable’s position if valid. Goal tiles require a selectable occupying them to win.

Movement is blocked by walls; CLICK can teleport/move the currently selected piece to the clicked tile if valid. To win, the selectable piece must occupy the goal tiles; since only one goal region exists, getting any selectable onto that goal should trigger WIN. P cannot move, so use CLICK to place it onto the goal area.

CLICK attempts to teleport/move the currently selected piece to the clicked pixel position only if that destination is valid (not inside walls / within allowed region). Arrow MOVE shifts by 3 pixels (1 cell) but is blocked by adjacent walls here. To win, a selectable must occupy the goal; likely need to CLICK directly onto the goal with the selected piece.

With 0 steps remaining, no further movement or selection changes are possible, so the level cannot be completed from this state. CLICK previously acted like a teleport/select-to-position when the target was a valid selectable position, but attempts to click near the goal did nothing, likely due to walls/invalid target; regardless, the step budget is exhausted so no action can be taken now.

Agent explained goal and mechanics.

understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to win each level by getting every selectable piece onto the goal tiles marked with '+' at the same time; a level only reports WIN when all goal tiles are simultaneously occupied by selectable pieces, and completing the run requires achieving WIN at the end.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Turns consume a limited step budget. MOVE_LEFT/RIGHT/UP/DOWN moves the currently selected piece in that direction, with movement constrained by obstacles/walls (blocked moves result in no position change). CLICK [x,y] functions as a direct reposition/teleport of the selected piece to the clicked coordinate when that destination is valid; repeated clicks showed the piece snapping between two reachable coordinates ([12,15] and [18,21]), while clicks at [36,18] and [37,18] had no effect, implying those targets were blocked, out of bounds, or otherwise invalid. Only one piece was being controlled at a time (the 'selected piece'), and actions always applied to that selected piece.
